title: Enable Codex
intro: One-click switch the model Codex actually calls — a replacement for ccswitch. Two mechanisms are offered:

modesInfo.applyFile: <1>Write files and enable</1>: physically writes ~/.codex/auth.json and ~/.codex/config.toml, same as ccswitch. <3>Requires a Codex restart</3>. Original files are auto-backed up and one-click restorable.
modesInfo.runtimeOverride: <1>Runtime override only</1>: stores the active (provider, model) inside mimo2codex; selectProvider prefers it during routing. <3>No Codex restart needed</3>, but Codex must already connect to mimo2codex (first-time setup still needs the option above).

state.title: Current state
state.codexDirHelp: Any absolute path works (it doesn't need to exist yet). Changes take effect immediately — no restart required. Existing backups stay in the old dir.

owner.external: external (real OpenAI key or another tool)

targets.title: Available combinations
targets.externalWarn: Your current ~/.codex/auth.json was not written by mimo2codex (probably a real OpenAI login or another tool). 'Write files and enable' will back it up first, then overwrite — restore is always available.
targets.applyTitle: Writes ~/.codex/auth.json and config.toml — requires Codex restart
targets.overrideTitle: Persist active model in settings DB — no Codex restart needed
targets.overrideDisabledTitle: Provider has no API key — cannot be used as runtime override
targets.probeTitle: Send a minimal request to verify the link (key / baseUrl / model id)

override.title: Runtime override
override.empty: No runtime override set. Click 'Runtime override only' on any row above to enable.

backup.title: Backup & restore
backup.intro: Each 'Write files and enable' auto-backs up the current auth.json + config.toml. The most recent 10 are kept; 🔒-marked backups (the first one captured when overwriting your real Codex config) are kept permanently and won't be pruned.
backup.empty: No backups yet. The first 'Write files and enable' will produce one automatically.

type.preservedTitle: Captured the first time we overwrote an external auth.json; kept permanently

backup.deletePreservedTitle: Locked — deleting would permanently lose the original config

msg.applied: Wrote {{provider}} / {{model}}. {{note}}Fully quit and restart Codex for the new config to take effect.
msg.appliedPreserved: Backed up your original Codex config (ts={{ts}}, 🔒 kept permanently — won't be pruned by later switches).
msg.overrideSet: Runtime override set to {{provider}} / {{model}}. No Codex restart needed — the next request will pick it up.
msg.overrideCleared: Runtime override cleared — routing falls back to default behavior.
msg.restored: Restored from backup ts={{ts}}. Restart Codex for the old config to take effect.

confirm.applyP1: Your current ~/.codex/auth.json was not written by mimo2codex and may contain your real OpenAI login.
confirm.applyP2: Proceeding will back it up (auth.json.bak.<ts>) and write a mimo2codex placeholder key. You can restore from 'Backup & restore' below.
confirm.restoreBody: The current auth.json + config.toml will NOT be backed up again.
confirm.restoreMissingAuth: auth.json (did not exist at the time → restore will delete the current auth.json)
confirm.restoreMissingToml: config.toml (did not exist at the time → restore will delete the current config.toml)
confirm.deletePreserved:
This is a '🔒 Original Codex config' backup — deleting it permanently removes the ability to restore your initial real Codex config.

Proceed?
confirm.deleteNormal: Delete backup ts={{ts}}? This is irreversible.
